name: Healthcare First Credit Union
description_short: Healthcare First Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving health care employees with loans, savings accounts, mobile banking, and branch services across Pennsylvania.
description_long:
Healthcare First Credit Union (HFCU) is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union that specifically serves employees in the healthcare industry. The organization has been providing financial services to this specialized workforce and operates multiple branches across Pennsylvania, including locations in Johnstown, Altoona, Duncansville, and an upcoming Ebensburg branch opening in Spring 2026. As a credit union, HFCU is NCUA-insured and operates under the cooperative financial model rather than shareholder-driven banking.

HFCU offers a range of consumer financial products including auto loans, personal loans, online banking, mobile banking with dedicated card management features, and savings programs. They provide loan preapproval services, require standard income documentation (recent paystubs or proof of social security/pension income), and offer multiple application methods including online home banking, in-person at branches, email, and fax submission. Recently, they introduced a Round Up Savings program that allows members to automatically save money through everyday spending. The organization actively promotes special offers, such as their current 0.25% rate discount on new loans to celebrate their Ebensburg branch opening.

What distinguishes HFCU is their mission-focused approach to serving healthcare employees specifically, rather than operating as a general-purpose bank. They invest in member experience through technology updates (such as their new "My Cards" mobile feature) and physical expansion (Ebensburg branch), and they maintain a rebranding initiative that reflects their current identity and future direction. Their loan application process is streamlined through multiple submission channels, and they provide transparency with a loan calculator tool on their website.

HFCU is well-suited for healthcare workers seeking member-owned financial services with competitive loan rates and personalized service. The main caveat is that membership eligibility appears limited to healthcare employees, which restricts access to the general public. Limited detailed product information is available on their homepage—specific rates, fees, and eligibility criteria require direct contact or account login. Their physical footprint, while growing, remains geographically concentrated in Pennsylvania.
services: Auto loans with competitive rates and online rate calculator
Personal loans with preapproval, online application, and 0.25% promotional rate discount
Online home banking with loan application and account management features
Mobile banking app with "My Cards" card management functionality
Loan preapproval with income documentation (paystubs or social security/pension proof)
Round Up Savings program for automated savings during debit card transactions
Share accounts and savings products (credit union membership accounts)
Multiple loan submission channels (online, email, fax, in-person at branches)
Loan calculator tool for payment and rate estimation
Physical branch services at Johnstown, Altoona, Duncansville locations
Accessibility services and support (accessibility page available)
Customer service via phone (888-296-8728) and email (loans@healthcarefirstcu.org)
pros: Member-owned credit union structure with NCUA insurance protection, not shareholder-driven
Mission-focused on healthcare employee financial needs with specialized understanding of this workforce
Multiple loan application channels including online home banking, email, fax, and in-person submission
Active expansion with new Ebensburg branch opening Spring 2026 and recent logo/brand refresh
Recent innovation with Round Up Savings program for automated savings during spending
New mobile banking features including dedicated "My Cards" card management functionality
Current promotional loan rate discount (0.25% off) to support member borrowing
Streamlined loan preapproval process with clear income documentation requirements
cons: Membership appears limited to healthcare employees, restricting access for general consumers
Limited specific product information on website—APRs, fees, and full eligibility criteria not clearly disclosed
Physical branch network concentrated in Pennsylvania (Johnstown, Altoona, Duncansville, Ebensburg), limiting in-person access for non-local members
Recent branch hour changes (effective April 2026) may reduce convenient access at Franklin Street and Duncansville locations
No publicly available information about online-only account options or remote membership eligibility
best_for: Healthcare industry employees seeking competitive loan rates from a mission-aligned credit union
Pennsylvania-based healthcare workers wanting personalized service and community financial institution support
Members looking for automated savings programs integrated with everyday spending (Round Up Savings)
Healthcare employees planning major purchases or debt consolidation with access to rate discounts
diagnosis: Healthcare First Credit Union is best for healthcare industry employees in Pennsylvania seeking member-owned credit union services with competitive loan rates and personalized service. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility appears restricted to healthcare workers, and detailed product rates and fees are not publicly disclosed on their website—direct contact is required for specific pricing and eligibility confirmation.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
